scenario: Northwind Retailer ships a Camunda 8.8 DMN model that classifies claim severity for 3200 orders per day. The architect renames the decision element id to align with team conventions for the new release, and must pick a value the deployment will accept and that also lets a dependent decision keep referencing the result through the DRG.
question: Which decision id value is permitted by the DMN id naming rule for this decision element?

options.text: The decision id value claim-handler is set on the decision element so it can be referenced by other DMN files later
The decision id value claimHandler is set on the decision element so it can be referenced by other DMN files later
The decision id value claim handler is set on the decision element so it can be referenced by other DMN files later
The decision id value claim.handler is set on the decision element so it can be referenced by other DMN files later

a.text: Incorrect. The kebab-case form contains the dash operator, which the DMN id rule forbids; if used, the decision result cannot be accessed in a dependent decision.

b.text: Correct. The camelCase form is alphanumeric only and is explicitly recommended by the docs for word combinations alongside snake_case, so the deployment accepts it.

c.text: Incorrect. Whitespace is one of the special characters the docs forbid in the decision id; using it would prevent the result from being accessed by a dependent decision.

d.text: Incorrect. The dot is a special symbol that the docs forbid in the decision id; the result of the decision would then be unreachable from a dependent decision.

explanation: The DMN docs allow any alphanumeric string plus the underscore in the decision id; camelCase and snake_case are recommended for word combinations, while kebab-case, whitespace, and other symbols are not allowed because they break references from dependent decisions.
